ACC.summary: The ACC is a Power Four league that carries 6.82% of national title equity across 1000 simulations. Members fill 8.11% of all playoff field slots — a meaningful slice of the 12-team bracket. Miami Hurricanes is a heavy favorite at 46.9% conference title odds. The next tier includes SMU Mustangs (13.2%), Louisville Cardinals (11.1%), Clemson Tigers (7.4%). Miami Hurricanes averages 10.31 wins and reaches the playoff field in 54.9% of simulations. Miami Hurricanes is the league's top national title threat at 5.05% championship odds, and the top three teams hold 71.2% of the conference's conf title odds. Schedules are demanding: member teams average +0.6 strength of schedule in the model. Stanford Cardinal brings up the rear at 3.76 average wins.

American.summary: The American is a Group of 6 league in the 2026 preseason model, with a member in the playoff field in 35.7% of 1000 simulations. The league accounts for 0.09% of national title equity. The league championship race is wide open: Navy Midshipmen leads at 19.0%, but no team clears a quarter of the sims. The next tier includes South Florida (15.1%), East Carolina (13.9%), UTSA Roadrunners (13.1%). Navy Midshipmen averages 8.12 wins and reaches the playoff field in 4.6% of simulations. South Florida is the league's top national title threat at 0.02% championship odds. That 6.8% playoff rate is what makes the American a live Group of 6 autobid path in this model. When a American team earns an autobid, it is usually South Florida (6.8% playoff field rate). Charlotte 49ers brings up the rear at 1.90 average wins.
